chunk:
CHUNK:014/021
And, if Petra is unharmed, and I mean, not a single hair missing from her head kind of unharmed, then I will help you.\u201d \u201cYou\u2019ll help me?\u201d \u201cI will,\u201d Gaba said, and he nodded. \u201cYou have my word.\u201d \u201cAnd if the lovely Petra loses one of her pretty hairs?\u201d Rachael took a breath. \u201cWhat then, Mr Police Man?\u201d Gaba said nothing and Rachael took another breath, deeper than the last, before exhaling with a curse. \u201cRight,\u201d she said. \u201cI get it.\u201d \u201cI hope so,\u201d Gaba said. I might have been a few steps behind getting it, but when Rachael pushed me towards the stairs, Gaba took another exaggerated step backwards, catching my eye once more as we passed, and whispering something in Danish. \u201cLightweight,\u201d he said, although I might have been mistaken. Gaba twisted his lips into a brief smile when I frowned at him, and then turned quickly as Saarullik and Taateraaq jogged down the corridor and burst into the lounge area. Gaba snapped his fingers and shouted for them to Stand down! just as Rachael hurried me down the stairs. \u201cWhere are we going?\u201d I said, focusing on my feet and not falling, as Rachael picked up the pace. \u201cWe need a boat, honey, and someone who can drive it.\u201d \u201cI can drive a boat,\u201d I said. \u201cOf course you can.\u201d Rachael laughed and said, \u201cI really lucked out when I chose you for a playmate, didn\u2019t I?\u201d \u201cYou sure did,\u201d I said, catching the Americanism and rolling my eyes so that Atii, in the distance, somewhere in my mind, didn\u2019t have to. \u201cWe\u2019re going to make a good team, Petra,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cNow, be a good girl, and find us a boat.\u201d \n 11 Girl. Rachael was in her late twenties. At most, she was thirty. But then she did have both my guns, and I decided not to argue about age, or challenge her when she called me girl, or honey. As long as she was holding the guns, she could call me whatever she wanted to, as I slipped into the role of the compliant hostage, keeping in line, and doing everything she asked of me. I had limits, of course, but when I glimpsed Rachael\u2019s eyes, I saw something there that suggested she was driven by some greater plan, and I was literally a means to an end. Ordinarily, I might have worried about that, but I believed Rachael when she said she had no intentions of hurting anything but my pride. I was going to hold her to that. \u201cWhich one, honey?\u201d Rachael said as we walked down the zigzag ladder to the floating dock. I was surprised it was still in place, and even more surprised when Rachael pointed at the three black-hulled Zodiac boats, prompting me to choose one so we could get the hell out of here, as she put it. \u201cThe black one on the left,\u201d I said, flashing Rachael a cheeky smile. \u201cCute,\u201d Rachael said, nodding for me to go ahead. I climbed over the side of the Zodiac, appreciating the size and feel of a more robust boat than the rental, and then helped Rachael over the side. I unhooked the painter and pushed off the dock, letting the Zodiac drift as I fiddled with the outboard motors \u2013 plural. There were two. As Rachael settled on one of the seats, I glanced up at the cruise ship, half expecting to see Gaba watching me from the deck, but then gave up when I guessed he would be watching, but that he would be doing it discretely. I took my time with the motors, wondering if Rachael knew that I was stalling, or if she realised I had pushed us away from the dock before starting the engine, to prevent her from untying the other two boats. I assumed that Gaba and my colleagues would use them to follow us, just as soon as we had put a little distance between us and the cruise ship. \u201cHow come you know all this stuff?\u201d Rachael asked, resting the MP5 on her knees. \u201cBoats, you mean?\u201d \u201cSure.\u201d Rachael nodded. \u201cThey teach you this at police school?\u201d \u201cAt the academy?\u201d I shook my head. \u201cNot so much. That comes later. They teach us how to shoot, how to drive, and\u2026\u201d I gave Rachael a sterner look and said, \u201cHow to take care of ourselves.\u201d \u201cRight,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cAnd how\u2019s that working for you?\u201d I shrugged, said something about still working on it, and then turned my back on her to start the motors. Each motor gave a satisfying growl when I started them, and I sat behind the wheel, ready to click out of neutral and open them up once Rachael told me where we were going. \u201cThe village starting with the letter \u2018q\u2019,\u201d she said. \u201cQassiarsuk?\u201d \u201cRight.\u201d Rachael pointed at the settlement and said, \u201cThat one.\u201d The Zodiac lurched forward as I clicked into gear and opened the throttle. Rachael clung to her seat, and I bit my lip to stifle a laugh. The Zodiac had a bit more oomph to it than the hunter\u2019s dinghy Gaba had rented, and I tucked the image of Rachael slipping off her seat when I throttled up as a possible trick to use when I made my escape. Escape? Who was I kidding? I was too curious to escape. And when I thought about Gaba\u2019s reaction on the ship, the boats ready and waiting for us on the dock, and the fact that we had a short window of a few hours before the Americans arrived, I was almost convinced that escape was the last thing anyone wanted me to do, and certainly not Rachael. She pulled back her sleeve to reveal a slim watch on her wrist and I wondered whose schedule she was working to, and how much time she thought we had left.
compressed_context:
GABA: "IF PETRA UNHARMED (NO HAIR MISSING) ⇒ I HELP YOU."
RACHAEL: "YOU HELP ME?"
GABA: "I WILL. YOU HAVE MY WORD."
RACHAEL: "IF PETRA LOSES 1 HAIR ⇒ WHAT THEN, MR POLICE MAN?"
RACHAEL: "RIGHT. I GET IT."
GABA: "I HOPE SO."
NARRATOR: LAGS; RACHAEL PUSHES ME → STAIRS.
GABA: STEP-BACK EXAGGERATED; EYE-CONTACT; WHISPERS(DAN): "LIGHTWEIGHT"
NARRATOR: FROWNS; GABA SMILES BRIEFLY.
SAARULLIK ∧ TAATERAAQ: JOG IN; BURST INTO LOUNGE.
GABA: SNAP-FINGERS ∧ SHOUTS: "STAND DOWN!"
RACHAEL: HURRIES NARRATOR DOWN STAIRS.
NARRATOR: "WHERE ARE WE GOING?" (EYES ON FEET)
RACHAEL: "WE NEED A BOAT, HONEY, AND A DRIVER."
NARRATOR: "I CAN DRIVE A BOAT."
RACHAEL: "OF COURSE YOU CAN."
RACHAEL: LAUGHS ∧ SAYS: "I LUCKED OUT CHOOSING YOU AS PLAYMATE, DIDN'T I?"
NARRATOR: "YOU SURE DID." (ROLLS EYES)
RACHAEL: "WE MAKE A GOOD TEAM, PETRA."
RACHAEL: "NOW BE A GOOD GIRL ∧ FIND US A BOAT."
NARRATOR: RACHAEL AGE≈25–30 ∧ HOLDS MY GUNS ⇒ I CEDE ON AGE ∧ TERMS.
NARRATOR: ASSUMES COMPLIANT-HOSTAGE ROLE; OBEYS REQUESTS; MAINTAINS LIMITS.
NARRATOR: RACHAEL'S EYES ⇒ GREATER-PLAN; I AM MEANS-TO-END; THREAT=PRIDE.
RACHAEL: "WHICH ONE, HONEY?" (DESCEND ZIGZAG LADDER → FLOATING-DOCK)
NARRATOR: SURPRISED LADDERS STILL IN PLACE.
RACHAEL: POINTS AT 3 BLACK-HULLED ZODIACS ∧ PROMPTS CHOICE.
NARRATOR: CHOOSES LEFT BLACK ONE; FLASHES CHEEKY SMILE.
RACHAEL: "CUTE." (NODS)
NARRATOR: CLIMBS INTO ZODIAC; NOTICES ROBUST VS RENTAL; HELPS RACHAEL IN.
NARRATOR: UNHOOKS PAINTER ∧ PUSHES OFF; ZODIAC DRIFTS; I STALL ENGINE; 2 OUTBOARD-MOTORS.
RACHAEL: SETTLES ON SEAT.
NARRATOR: LOOKS AT CRUISE-SHIP DECK; EXPECTS DISCREET GABA WATCH; CEASES LOOK.
NARRATOR: PUSHES US AWAY BEFORE START (PREVENTS UNTYING OTHER BOATS).
NARRATOR: ASSUMES GABA ∧ TEAM FOLLOW IN 2 OTHER ZODIACS AFTER DISTANCE.
RACHAEL: "HOW COME YOU KNOW ALL THIS STUFF?" (MP5 ON KNEES)
NARRATOR: "BOATS, YOU MEAN?"
RACHAEL: "SURE."
RACHAEL: "THEY TEACH THIS AT POLICE SCHOOL?"
NARRATOR: "AT ACADEMY?"
NARRATOR: "NOT SO MUCH. COMES LATER. THEY TEACH SHOOTING, DRIVING ∧ SELF-CARE."
RACHAEL: "RIGHT. AND HOW'S THAT WORKING?"
NARRATOR: SHRUGS; SAYS STILL WORKING ON IT.
NARRATOR: TURNS → STARTS MOTORS; EACH GROWLS; SITS BEHIND WHEEL; AWAITS GEAR.
RACHAEL: "VILLAGE STARTING WITH 'Q'."
NARRATOR: "QASSIARSUK?"
RACHAEL: "RIGHT." (POINTS)
NARRATOR: CLICK GEAR ∧ THROTTLE OPEN ⇒ ZODIAC MOVES FORWARD.
RACHAEL: CLINGS TO SEAT; NARRATOR BITES LIP TO STIFLE LAUGH.
NARRATOR: ZODIAC POWER > HUNTER-DINGHY; IMAGINES ESCAPE TRICK (RACHAEL SLIPPING).
NARRATOR: TOO CURIOUS TO ESCAPE.
NARRATOR: CONSIDERS GABA REACTION ∧ DOCK BOATS READY ∧ AMERICANS ETA≈FEW HRS ∴ ESCAPE UNDESIRABLE.
RACHAEL: PULLS SLEEVE → SLIM WATCH VISIBLE.
NARRATOR: WONDER WHOSE SCHEDULE SHE FOLLOWS ∧ TIME LEFT.
